description: To swallow: food, medicine, the words one thinks better of. The downward completion of eating and drinking.
sound_symbolism: 'no'–'lu' descends into the deep, 'ha' is the open throat it passes through.
grammatical_notes: Transitive: 'mia nepha noluha.' (I swallow the medicine.) Figurative: 'mia lo phelui to noluha.' (I swallowed my words.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The swallowed word is restraint's most bodily metaphor — the verb serves the figurative wisdom as well as the meal.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Medicine's oldest instruction: swallow this. Healing needed the verb before it needed the pharmacy.
